words.english: I hope you arrive on time
I doubt he is right
It is important that you study
I am glad you are here
Hopefully we can go out
I recommend that you speak calmly
It is necessary for him or her to exercise

words.spanish: No creo que sepan la respuesta
words.english: I do not think they know the answer
I want you to listen to me
I am afraid it might rain

words.spanish: Busco un médico que sea paciente
words.english: I am looking for a doctor who is patient
We need a plan that works
Before you leave
So that you understand better
Even if it is difficult
Maybe he or she will come tomorrow
It is strange that he or she does not reply
It worries me that you drive while tired

words.spanish: Insisto en que me digas la verdad
words.english: I insist that you tell me the truth
I prefer that we take the train
I suggest that you all review the contract

words.spanish: Es mejor que lo hagas hoy
words.english: It is better that you do it today

words.spanish: No es seguro que podamos entrar
words.english: It is not certain that we can get in

words.spanish: Ojalá que el jefe nos dé permiso
words.english: Hopefully the boss gives us permission
It surprises me that they know so much

words.spanish: Es posible que ella vaya sola
words.english: It is possible that she goes alone
They want me to be present

words.spanish: Dudo que el informe esté listo
words.english: I doubt the report is ready
It is advisable that you rest

words.spanish: Vale la pena que lo intentes
words.english: It is worth trying
To be patient
To be available
To get to the point
To be patient
To make an appointment
To be able to attend
To know how to solve problems
To follow up
The recommendation
The doubt
The emotion
The wish

dialog.speaker: Marta
dialog.spanish: Javier, tienes mala cara. Espero que llames al médico hoy mismo.
dialog.english: Javier, you look unwell. I hope you call the doctor today.

dialog.speaker: Javier
dialog.spanish: Sí, pero dudo que me den una cita tan rápido. Siempre están ocupados.
dialog.english: Yes, but I doubt they will give me an appointment that quickly. They are always busy.

dialog.speaker: Marta
dialog.spanish: Es importante que les expliques que tienes fiebre desde hace tres días y que no puedes dormir.
dialog.english: It is important that you explain to them that you have had a fever for three days and that you cannot sleep.

dialog.speaker: Javier
dialog.spanish: Me alegra que me ayudes, porque no creo que yo esté pensando con claridad.
dialog.english: I am glad you are helping me, because I do not think I am thinking clearly.

dialog.speaker: Marta
dialog.spanish: Recomiendo que no vayas a la oficina mañana y que descanses esta noche.
dialog.english: I recommend that you do not go to the office tomorrow and that you rest tonight.

dialog.speaker: Javier
dialog.spanish: Ojalá que la doctora me diga que no es nada grave.
dialog.english: Hopefully the doctor tells me it is nothing serious.

questions.question: ¿Por qué insiste Marta en que Javier llame al médico hoy?
options: Porque quiere cambiar de trabajo
Porque lleva varios días con fiebre y no duerme bien
Porque necesita pedir vacaciones
Porque tiene que viajar mañana
questions.answer: Porque lleva varios días con fiebre y no duerme bien

questions.question: ¿Qué duda tiene Javier sobre la clínica?
options: Que esté cerrada por reformas
Que la doctora no hable español
Que le den una cita tan rápido
Que el seguro ya esté pagado
questions.answer: Que le den una cita tan rápido

questions.question: ¿Qué espera Javier al final de la conversación?
options: Que le receten antibióticos muy fuertes
Que la doctora le diga que no es nada grave
Que Marta vaya a la oficina por él
Que cancelen todas sus reuniones
questions.answer: Que la doctora le diga que no es nada grave

conversations.topic: Proyecto de trabajo con plazos ajustados

dialog.speaker: Elena
dialog.spanish: Espero que tengas un momento para revisar la propuesta antes de la reunión con el cliente.
dialog.english: I hope you have a moment to review the proposal before the meeting with the client.

dialog.speaker: Ricardo
dialog.spanish: Claro, aunque dudo que el presupuesto alcance para todas las ideas que presentamos.
dialog.english: Of course, although I doubt the budget will be enough for all the ideas we presented.

dialog.speaker: Elena
dialog.spanish: Es necesario que hagamos una versión más simple y que demos prioridad a las funciones básicas.
dialog.english: It is necessary that we make a simpler version and give priority to the basic features.

dialog.speaker: Ricardo
dialog.spanish: Me sorprende que el cliente quiera cambios tan grandes en tan poco tiempo.
dialog.english: It surprises me that the client wants such big changes in so little time.

dialog.speaker: Elena
dialog.spanish: No creo que acepten otro retraso, así que sugiero que les mostremos un calendario realista.
dialog.english: I do not think they will accept another delay, so I suggest that we show them a realistic schedule.

dialog.speaker: Ricardo
dialog.spanish: Está bien; ojalá que valoren la honestidad y que podamos cerrar el acuerdo hoy.
dialog.english: All right; hopefully they value honesty and we can close the deal today.

questions.question: ¿Por qué propone Elena una versión más simple del proyecto?
options: Porque el cliente canceló la reunión
Porque el presupuesto quizá no alcance para todo
Porque nadie entiende las funciones básicas
Porque Ricardo quiere empezar de cero
questions.answer: Porque el presupuesto quizá no alcance para todo

questions.question: ¿Qué le preocupa a Elena sobre el cliente?
options: Que no acepte otro retraso
Que cambie de oficina
Que pida una rebaja mayor
Que viaje sin avisar

questions.question: ¿Qué espera Ricardo al terminar la reunión?
options: Que el cliente contrate a otro equipo
Que valoren la honestidad y cierren el acuerdo
Que suspendan el proyecto por un mes
Que Elena hable sola con el cliente
questions.answer: Que valoren la honestidad y cierren el acuerdo

dialog.speaker: Sofía
dialog.spanish: Ojalá que mamá y papá puedan venir a la graduación el sábado.
dialog.english: Hopefully mom and dad can come to the graduation on Saturday.

dialog.speaker: Diego
dialog.spanish: Yo también lo espero, pero no creo que consigan vuelos baratos a estas alturas.
dialog.english: I hope so too, but I do not think they will find cheap flights this late.

dialog.speaker: Sofía
dialog.spanish: Me pone triste que vivan tan lejos, aunque me alegra que siempre intenten acompañarnos.
dialog.english: It makes me sad that they live so far away, although I am glad they always try to be with us.

dialog.speaker: Diego
dialog.spanish: Es posible que viajen en autobús nocturno si no encuentran otra opción.
dialog.english: It is possible they will travel on an overnight bus if they do not find another option.

dialog.speaker: Sofía
dialog.spanish: Es importante que lleguen descansados; no quiero que pasen toda la noche incómodos.
dialog.english: It is important that they arrive rested; I do not want them to spend the whole night uncomfortable.

dialog.speaker: Diego
dialog.spanish: Tienes razón. Recomiendo que les enviemos dinero para que elijan el viaje más cómodo.
dialog.english: You are right. I recommend that we send them money so they can choose the most comfortable trip.

questions.question: ¿Qué pone triste a Sofía?
options: Que la graduación sea muy temprano
Que sus padres vivan tan lejos
Que Diego no quiera ayudarlos
Que el autobús salga de otra ciudad
questions.answer: Que sus padres vivan tan lejos

questions.question: ¿Qué alternativa de transporte menciona Diego?
options: Un taxi compartido
Un tren regional
Un autobús nocturno
Un vuelo con escala

questions.question: ¿Qué solución recomienda Diego para ayudar a los padres?
options: Enviarles dinero para un viaje más cómodo
Cancelar la graduación de este año
Reservarles un hotel más barato
Pedirle ayuda a un vecino
questions.answer: Enviarles dinero para un viaje más cómodo

dialog.speaker: Carlos
dialog.spanish: Me alegra que quieras ayudar en la campaña del barrio este fin de semana.
dialog.english: I am glad you want to help in the neighborhood campaign this weekend.

dialog.speaker: Laura
dialog.spanish: Claro, pero dudo que tengamos suficientes voluntarios para limpiar el parque y repartir comida.
dialog.english: Of course, but I doubt we will have enough volunteers to clean the park and distribute food.

dialog.speaker: Carlos
dialog.spanish: Por eso es importante que hables con tus compañeros de la universidad hoy mismo.
dialog.english: That is why it is important that you speak with your classmates today.

dialog.speaker: Laura
dialog.spanish: Lo haré. También sugiero que pidamos al supermercado local que nos done agua y fruta.
dialog.english: I will. I also suggest that we ask the local supermarket to donate water and fruit.

dialog.speaker: Carlos
dialog.spanish: Excelente idea. No creo que la gente se anime tanto si no ofrecemos algo básico para los voluntarios.
dialog.english: Excellent idea. I do not think people will be as willing if we do not offer something basic for the volunteers.

dialog.speaker: Laura
dialog.spanish: Ojalá que todo salga bien y que más vecinos se unan cuando vean el esfuerzo.
dialog.english: Hopefully everything goes well and more neighbors join when they see the effort.

questions.question: ¿Por qué quiere Carlos que Laura hable hoy con sus compañeros?
options: Porque necesita vender entradas
Porque faltan voluntarios para la campaña
Porque deben cambiar la fecha del evento
Porque el supermercado está cerrado
questions.answer: Porque faltan voluntarios para la campaña

questions.question: ¿Qué donación propone Laura pedir?
options: Mesas y sillas
Guantes y escobas
Agua y fruta
Libros y cuadernos

questions.question: Según Carlos, ¿qué puede animar más a los voluntarios?
options: Ofrecer algo básico durante la jornada
Reducir el trabajo a una sola hora
Hacer la campaña en otro barrio
Suspender la limpieza del parque
questions.answer: Ofrecer algo básico durante la jornada

sentences.spanish: Laura llevaba semanas con migrañas, así que su hermana insistió en que pidiera una cita con una especialista.
sentences.english: Laura had been dealing with migraines for weeks, so her sister insisted that she make an appointment with a specialist.

sentences.spanish: Laura dudaba que el seguro cubriera la consulta, pero le alegró que la clínica respondiera el mismo día.
sentences.english: Laura doubted that her insurance would cover the visit, but she was glad the clinic replied the same day.

sentences.spanish: La recepcionista recomendó que llegara temprano y que llevara todos sus análisis anteriores.
sentences.english: The receptionist recommended that she arrive early and bring all her previous test results.

sentences.spanish: Antes de que saliera de casa, su madre le recordó que comiera algo ligero y que tomara agua.
sentences.english: Before she left home, her mother reminded her to eat something light and drink water.

sentences.spanish: Ojalá que la doctora encontrara una solución simple, pensó Laura mientras esperaba en la sala.
sentences.english: Hopefully the doctor would find a simple solution, Laura thought while waiting in the room.

sentences.spanish: Al final, Laura sintió alivio porque era posible que el tratamiento funcionara sin interrumpir su trabajo.
sentences.english: In the end, Laura felt relieved because it was possible the treatment would work without interrupting her job.

sentences.spanish: El equipo esperaba que el cliente aceptara una propuesta más modesta después de varias semanas de cambios.
sentences.english: The team hoped the client would accept a more modest proposal after several weeks of changes.

sentences.spanish: Ricardo dudaba que el presupuesto alcanzara, así que pidió que revisaran cada gasto con mucho detalle.
sentences.english: Ricardo doubted the budget would be enough, so he asked that they review every expense in detail.

sentences.spanish: A Elena le molestaba que el cliente cambiara de opinión a última hora, pero sabía que convenía mantener la calma.
sentences.english: Elena was bothered that the client kept changing their mind at the last minute, but she knew it was wise to stay calm.

sentences.spanish: Antes de que empezara la reunión, ambos acordaron hablar con total honestidad.
sentences.english: Before the meeting started, they both agreed to speak with complete honesty.

sentences.spanish: Les alegró que el cliente valorara el calendario realista en lugar de exigir promesas imposibles.
sentences.english: They were glad the client valued the realistic timeline instead of demanding impossible promises.

sentences.spanish: Al salir, pensaron que valía la pena que el equipo siguiera trabajando con esa misma transparencia.
sentences.english: When they left, they thought it was worth it for the team to keep working with that same transparency.

sentences.spanish: Sofía soñaba con que sus padres estuvieran presentes el día de su graduación universitaria.
sentences.english: Sofía dreamed that her parents would be present on the day of her university graduation.

sentences.spanish: Aunque no creía que encontraran vuelos baratos, le emocionaba que siguieran buscando opciones.
sentences.english: Although she did not think they would find cheap flights, she was moved that they kept looking for options.

sentences.spanish: Diego sugirió que les enviaran dinero para que viajaran con más comodidad.
sentences.english: Diego suggested that they send them money so they could travel more comfortably.

sentences.spanish: Su madre pidió que nadie cambiara los planes hasta confirmar el horario del autobús nocturno.
sentences.english: Her mother asked that no one change the plans until they confirmed the overnight bus schedule.

sentences.spanish: A Sofía le alegró muchísimo que, al final, sus padres pudieran llegar antes de la ceremonia.
sentences.english: Sofía was thrilled that, in the end, her parents were able to arrive before the ceremony.

sentences.spanish: Cuando los vio entrar, sintió que todo el esfuerzo había valido la pena.
sentences.english: When she saw them walk in, she felt that every bit of effort had been worth it.

speaking.title: Práctica oral del presente de subjuntivo
speaking.description: Responde con deseos, dudas, emociones y recomendaciones usando el presente de subjuntivo.

question.spanish: Di en español: I hope you come early tomorrow.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I hope you come early tomorrow.

answer.spanish: Espero que vengas temprano mañana.
answer.english: I hope you come early tomorrow.

question.spanish: Di en español: I doubt he has enough money for the deposit.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I doubt he has enough money for the deposit.

answer.spanish: Dudo que él tenga suficiente dinero para el depósito.
answer.english: I doubt he has enough money for the deposit.

question.spanish: Di en español: It is important that you are honest with your team.
question.english: Say in Spanish: It is important that you are honest with your team.

answer.spanish: Es importante que seas honesto con tu equipo.
answer.english: It is important that you are honest with your team.

question.spanish: Di en español: I am glad you are feeling better after the treatment.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I am glad you are feeling better after the treatment.

answer.spanish: Me alegra que estés mejor después del tratamiento.
answer.english: I am glad you are feeling better after the treatment.

question.spanish: Di en español: Hopefully we can finish before six.
question.english: Say in Spanish: Hopefully we can finish before six.

answer.spanish: Ojalá que podamos terminar antes de las seis.
answer.english: Hopefully we can finish before six.

question.spanish: Di en español: I recommend that you go by subway to avoid traffic.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I recommend that you go by subway to avoid traffic.

answer.spanish: Recomiendo que vayan en metro para evitar el tráfico.
answer.english: I recommend that you go by subway to avoid traffic.

question.spanish: Di en español: It is necessary that he make a copy of the contract.
question.english: Say in Spanish: It is necessary that he make a copy of the contract.

answer.spanish: Es necesario que haga una copia del contrato.
answer.english: It is necessary that he make a copy of the contract.

question.spanish: Di en español: I do not think they know how to get there without a map.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I do not think they know how to get there without a map.

answer.spanish: No creo que sepan cómo llegar sin un mapa.
answer.english: I do not think they know how to get there without a map.

question.spanish: Di en español: I want you to give me a clear answer today.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I want you to give me a clear answer today.

answer.spanish: Quiero que me des una respuesta clara hoy.
answer.english: I want you to give me a clear answer today.

question.spanish: Di en español: I am afraid the child may not sleep well in the hospital.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I am afraid the child may not sleep well in the hospital.

answer.spanish: Temo que el niño no duerma bien en el hospital.
answer.english: I am afraid the child may not sleep well in the hospital.

question.spanish: Di en español: It is advisable that you drink water before running.
question.english: Say in Spanish: It is advisable that you drink water before running.

answer.spanish: Conviene que tomes agua antes de correr.
answer.english: It is advisable that you drink water before running.

question.spanish: Di en español: It is possible that she comes from Bogotá tonight.
question.english: Say in Spanish: It is possible that she comes from Bogotá tonight.

answer.spanish: Es posible que ella venga desde Bogotá esta noche.
answer.english: It is possible that she comes from Bogotá tonight.

question.spanish: Di en español: I am looking for an apartment that has a balcony and good light.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I am looking for an apartment that has a balcony and good light.

answer.spanish: Busco un apartamento que tenga balcón y buena luz.
answer.english: I am looking for an apartment that has a balcony and good light.

question.spanish: Di en español: I prefer that we are seated near the exit.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I prefer that we are seated near the exit.

answer.spanish: Prefiero que estemos sentados cerca de la salida.
answer.english: I prefer that we are seated near the exit.

question.spanish: Di en español: I insist that you tell him the whole truth.
question.english: Say in Spanish: I insist that you tell him the whole truth.

answer.spanish: Insisto en que le digas toda la verdad.
answer.english: I insist that you tell him the whole truth.

question.spanish: Di en español: Before you all leave, check the passports.
question.english: Say in Spanish: Before you all leave, check the passports.

answer.spanish: Antes de que salgan, revisen los pasaportes.
answer.english: Before you all leave, check the passports.

question.spanish: Di en español: So that you can concentrate, turn off the cell phone.
question.english: Say in Spanish: So that you can concentrate, turn off the cell phone.

answer.spanish: Para que puedas concentrarte, apaga el celular.
answer.english: So that you can concentrate, turn off the cell phone.

question.spanish: Di en español: It surprises me that the boss goes to the negotiation alone.
question.english: Say in Spanish: It surprises me that the boss goes to the negotiation alone.

answer.spanish: Me sorprende que el jefe vaya solo a la negociación.
answer.english: It surprises me that the boss goes to the negotiation alone.

question.spanish: Di en español: It is not certain that the store opens on Sunday.
question.english: Say in Spanish: It is not certain that the store opens on Sunday.

answer.spanish: No es seguro que la tienda abra en domingo.
answer.english: It is not certain that the store opens on Sunday.

question.spanish: Di en español: It is worth it for us to try one more time.
question.english: Say in Spanish: It is worth it for us to try one more time.

answer.spanish: Vale la pena que lo intentemos una vez más.
answer.english: It is worth it for us to try one more time.
